behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff: request builder -> API client payload. A pure unit test of a formatter can miss dropped labels during wiring/serialization. No real OS/network needed; stub transport is enough. Higher levels rejected: System is disqualified because behavior text only requires request content, not real network behavior; Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because correctness does not depend on model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Wire classifier with a fake API transport that captures outgoing payload; call classify with a taxonomy and assert captured request contains the full label set exactly once before dispatch.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: This is local normalization/validation logic at one object boundary; no inter-module failure is required to reproduce. Higher levels rejected: Integration/System/Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because behavior text is about mapping an out-of-taxonomy value to fallback, which can be reproduced with a direct function/object call.
behaviors.test_description: Call normalization/response-handling logic with taxonomy {legal, technical, financial, other} and raw label "medical"; assert result is "unknown".

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: Determinism of normalization is pure local logic; same input should produce same output. No real infra or model dependency needed. Higher levels rejected: Integration/System/Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because behavior explicitly scopes to normalization step with identical raw responses.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke normalization twice (or property-test many times) with the same raw response fixture and taxonomy; assert outputs are identical.

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires repeated live API calls and consistency of returned labels, which depends on real model/provider behavior and possible non-determinism. Mocked responses would always pass and hide real drift. Higher level rejected: Workflow is disqualified because this is repeated single-step classification calls, not a multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run N real classification calls for the same document/taxonomy, store provider raw responses + final labels, and assert label changes only when provider raw response changes.

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification: Real failure is at handoff between budget enforcement and dispatch: module must either truncate/reject and then dispatch (or not) correctly. A unit test of token math alone can miss mis-wiring that still sends over-budget payloads. Higher levels rejected: System is disqualified because real OS/network is unnecessary; Agentic/Workflow disqualified because this tests pre-dispatch infrastructure, not model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Use classifier wired with fake tokenizer/config and fake transport; exercise over-budget inputs and assert policy effect (truncate or reject) and dispatch behavior (payload size/dispatch suppression) matches configuration.

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: Behavior depends on real AI API execution and provider-reported usage metadata; stubs can mask true usage behavior. Per guidance, tests traversing real AI API should default Agentic unless purely local routing/retry checks. Here the assertion is about real provider usage outcomes. Higher level rejected: Workflow is disqualified because only one model call outcome is evaluated, not chained model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Execute real API calls for representative documents, capture provider-reported usage fields, and assert usage <= configured budget for each call.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is orchestration across retry policy, backoff timing, and API client response handling. In-process fake API/clock can reproduce 429 -> wait -> retry flow without real network. Higher levels rejected: System is disqualified because behavior does not require real network conditions, only correct handling of provider signal; Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because correctness is retry infrastructure, not model output or multi-step agent decisions.
behaviors.test_description: With fake transport returning rate-limit response then success, and controllable clock/sleeper, assert module waits per policy, retries, and returns successful label instead of surfacing error.
